steps.title: Recognize what the search method reveals
steps.content: Isla's choice to use a drone and search from the air—after not recognizing the geoglyph—reveals an implicit assumption about where the geoglyph must be. His change in approach suggests something about its location.

steps.title: Apply the search strategy to the choices
steps.content: If the geoglyph is in a location Isla hadn't come across while on the ground, an aerial search becomes logical—it provides access to areas his ground-level exploration had never covered. The other choices—extinct species, location in Germany, and creation timeline—don't explain why switching to an aerial vantage point would be a useful strategy for finding the geoglyph. That matches choice C — C is correct.
